MORNING.TITLE: Morning
MORNING.TEXT_1: On a warm summer morning in the city, people are waking up and getting ready for their day. The Sun is already shining brightly.

VEHICLE_TYPES.TITLE: Vehicle Types
VEHICLE_TYPES.TEXT_1: People use many different types of vehicles to get to where they are going. What kind of vehicles might people use? Cars, buses, motorcycles, scooters, and bicycles... Most of these vehicles [Factory + Car] burn gasoline or natural gas, called fossil fuels, to make them go.

SUNNY_DAY.TITLE: Sunny Day
SUNNY_DAY.TEXT_1: But on a warm sunny day when certain types of air pollution are already in the atmosphere, something else can form.

AIR.TITLE: Air
AIR.NARRATIVE: What's in the air around us?

NEARBY_FACTORIES.TITLE: Nearby Factories
NEARBY_FACTORIES.TEXT_1: Nearby factories also burn fossil fuels to make electricity that we use in our homes, schools, and workplaces. Click on the fossil fuels to see the electricity being produced!

BURNING_FUELS.TITLE: Burning Fuel
BURNING_FUELS.TEXT_1: Burning fossil fuels releases invisible gases into the air — carbon dioxide [CO2], nitrogen dioxide [NO2], volatile organic compounds (VOCs) [VOC], and other gases, too. Factories and vehicles [Factory + Car] also release solid particles of dust, carbon soot [C], and smoke that are so tiny they can float in the air for a long time.
BURNING_FUELS.FACTORY_BUBBLE: We burn fossil fuels to create energy for the city!
BURNING_FUELS.CAR_BUBBLE: Engines burn gasoline to make cars move!

OZONE_INGREDIENTS.TITLE: Ozone Ingredients
OZONE_INGREDIENTS.TEXT_1: Let’s gather the atmospheric “ingredients” needed to make this particular type of air pollution. We need: VOCs, which can come from car exhaust pipes, or from chemicals in paint or other industrial products; we also need nitrogen oxides like NO₂, which also come from car exhaust pipes and from factory emissions.

GATHER_INGREDIENTS.TITLE: Gathering Ingredients
GATHER_INGREDIENTS.TEXT_1: Create ozone by dragging VOC and NO₂ molecules to the smog cloud.
GATHER_INGREDIENTS.INGREDIENTS_GATHERED_BUBBLE: Great! Now that you have gathered the ingredients you can procced

AREA.PROMPT: Ozone pollution is worse in certain areas. Can you guess where this happens?
AREA.CORRECT: Correct! Ozone pollution is worse in urban areas!
AREA.INCORRECT: Actually, no! There is less ozone pollution in rural areas!

SEASON.PROMPT: Another factor that influences ozone pollution is time of year. When might pollution be worse?
SEASON.CORRECT: Correct! Ozone pollution is worse in the hot and sunny months!
SEASON.INCORRECT: Actually, no! Cool and dark months will have less ozone pollution!

CARS_FACTORIES.PROMPT: Density of cars and factories also makes an impact since these emit the ingredients of ozone. Knowing this, where might pollution be worse?
CARS_FACTORIES.CHOICE_LOTS: Areas with lots of cars and factories!
CARS_FACTORIES.CHOICE_FEW: Areas with fewer cars or factories!
CARS_FACTORIES.CORRECT: Correct! Ozone pollution is worse where there are many cars and/or factories!
CARS_FACTORIES.INCORRECT: Actually, no! Cars and factories produce the ingredients for ozone, so pollution is worse where there are more of them!

END.TITLE: End
END.TEXT_1: This is here for testing purposes
